papers.title: Software architectural support for tangible user interfaces in distributed, heterogeneous computing environments
papers.abstract: This research focuses on tools that support the development of tangible interaction-based applications for distributed computing environments. Applications built with these are capable utilizing heterogeneous resources interaction and can be reconfigured different contexts minimal code changes. Current trends in computing, especially areas such as computational science, scientific visualization computer supported collaborative work, foreshadow increasing complexity, distribution remoteness computation data. These imply interface developers must address concerns both design networked computing. In this dissertation, we present a software architecture supports separation concerns. Additionally, tangibles-based toolkit based is presented enables logic elements within user to mapped configurations vary number, type location given system.

papers.title: AABC: A Tool for Assessing Arduino Basic Coding Skills
papers.abstract: ABSTRACT In recent years, coding has become a useful component of education at all levels, leading to the emergence various programmable devices and platforms, such as Arduino. These tools offer students opportunities enhance their skills through hands‐on experiences or graphical simulations. However, literature lacks comprehensive instrument for evaluating code via technologies. To address this gap, study introduces “Assessing Arduino Basics in Coding” (AABC) tool. This tool was validated refined with 151 university students, who completed three experimental exercises followed by coding‐related questions. Students were divided into two groups. The first group implemented experiments physical–tangible boards, while second used interfaces virtual environment. analysis questionnaire scores underwent four steps. Initially, Item Response Theory employed discard questions resulting unscaled scores. Subsequently, Exploratory Factor Analysis identified factors corresponding exercises. Additionally, Confirmatory confirmed questionnaire's structure, indicating high reliability (χ 2 [74] = 74.5, p 0.463, CFI 0.995, TLI 0.994, RMSEA 0.00612, SRMR 0.0625). Lastly, measurement invariance testing demonstrated that AABC is unaffected user interface, suggesting its usability regardless interface used. Overall, provides reliable method basic circuits, contributing advancements education.

papers.title: Electronic Paper Computers: Interacting with Flexible Displays for Physical Manipulation of Digital Information
Designing the XR Medium: Five Rhetorical Perspectives for the Ecology of Human+Computer Networks
papers.abstract: <p>Cycles of invented digital media environments driven by the optimization feasibility technology, from personal computers to social media, while creating efficient systems, have also produced psychological and side effects including addiction, depression anxiety among users, erosion privacy fraying fabric. Escalating blending immersive technologies with advanced computation allows system makers produce not only human-computer interaction networks, but advanced, multi-minded human+computer (H+C) systems. papers.title: Dvoukanálový kontrolér krokových motorů
papers.venue: Brno University of Technology Digital Library (Brno University of Technology)
papers.abstract: Cílem této práce je vývoj dvoukanálového kontroléru krokových motorů. V rámci popsán jak elektroniky, tak příslušného software. Elektronika kontrolŕu založena na mikrokontroléru STM32F405 a driverů motorů vyráběných firmou Trinamic. Pro komunikaci s nadřazenými systémy implementován protokol CANOpen sběrnice I\\textsuperscript{2}C USB. byla navržena v software KiCAD and využívá čtyřvrstvého plošného spoje moderních výrobních technologií. Co se týká software, byl vyvinut firmware pro mikrokontrolér, ovládání kontroléru. Obě části využívají programovacího jazyka Rust, který zaměřuje bezpečnost pamětí, rychlost zero-cost abstrakce. Sekundárním cílem ukázat, lze tento programovací jazyk výhodou použít programování nízkoúrovňového embedded Firmware implementuje nezávislé řízení pohybu obou os to rychlostním, pozičním režimu zároveň bezpečnostní funkce případy selhání komunikace. Výsledný kontrolér by měl být použit rámcí výzkumné skupiny Robotiky Umělé Inteligence studenty Ústavu Automatizace FEKT VUT.

papers.title: Development of a Search Engine Tool for Visually Impaired Web Users
papers.abstract: A detailed walkthrough of the engineering process for development an accessible search engine tool. Contributions include a comprehensive literature review, assumptions, requirements, high-level design, implementation, and usability evaluations.

papers.title: GView: A Versatile Assistant for Security Researchers
papers.abstract: Cyber security attacks have become increasingly complex over time, with various phases of their kill chain, involving binaries, scripts, documents, executed commands, vulnerabilities, or network traffic. We propose a tool, GView, that is designed to investigate possible by providing guided analysis for file types using automatic artifact identification, extraction, coherent correlation &,inference, and meaningful & intuitive views at different levels granularity w.r.t. revealed information. papers.title: MinGLE: A Minimalist, Configurable, and Pedagogical Geant4 Application Template
papers.abstract: The Geant4 toolkit is the leading software for simulation of particle transport through matter, widely used in nuclear physics, high-energy and medical physics. However, initial learning curve new developers can be steep, often due to complexity experiment-specific nature many introductory examples. This paper introduces MinGLE (Mini Learning Example), a dedicated application template designed universal, flexible, educational starting point projects. achieves complete, functional kernel using fewer than 70 lines core C++ code. minimalism realized by leveraging contemporary features, including factory classes run management Text Geometry format detector definition. Furthermore, employs unique pedagogical structure, Git tags document incremental development eleven components, with each tagged version being fully compilable, executable, testable, providing clear, step-by-step resource.

papers.title: Exploring Reality-Based User Interfaces for Collaborative Information Seeking
papers.abstract: Information seeking activities such as searching the web or browsing a media library are often considered to be solitary experiences. papers.title: Prompt-Driven Development with Claude Code: Developing a TUI Framework for the Ring Programming Language
papers.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used in software development, yet their ability to generate and maintain large, multi-module systems through natural interaction remains insufficiently characterized. This study presents an empirical analysis of developing a 7420-line Terminal User Interface (TUI) framework for the Ring programming using prompt-driven workflow with Claude Code (Opus 4.5), employing iterative testing corrective feedback. The system was produced 107 prompts: 21 feature requests, 72 bug fix prompts, 9 prompts sharing information from documentation, 4 providing architectural guidance, 1 prompt dedicated generating documentation. Development progressed across five phases, Window Manager phase requiring most (35 prompts), followed by complex UI (25 prompts) control expansion (20 prompts). Bug-related covered redraw issues, event-handling faults, runtime errors, layout inconsistencies, while requests focused primarily on new widgets, window-manager capabilities, advanced components. Most were brief (mean ≈ 258 characters; median = 207 characters), reflecting highly which human role limited specifying requirements, validating behavior, issuing prompts—without writing any code manually. resulting contains 28 classes, 334 methods includes windowing subsystem, event-driven architecture, interactive hierarchical menus, grid tree components, tab controls, multi-window desktop environment. By combining quantitative qualitative assessment model this provides evidence that modern LLMs can preserve coherence iterations support construction libraries tools emerging languages, highlighting development as viable methodology within software-engineering practice.

papers.title: AnyBoard spillplattform - Et JavaScript rammeverk for å støtte utviklingingen av digital brettspill
papers.abstract: Et JavaScript-basert rammeverk, AnyBoard har blitt utviklet og ligger nå fritt tilgjengelig for hvem som helst å benytte seg av. Rammeverket forenkler utviklingen av digitale brettspill ved gjøre Bluetooth kommunikasjon brikker til Cordova-baserte applikasjoner.
